title: Fast and Slow Pointers
analogy: Imagine two runners on a circular track — the fast runner goes twice as fast. Eventually the fast runner laps the slow runner, proving the track has a loop.

overview.summary: The fast-and-slow pointer technique (Floyd's Tortoise and Hare) uses two pointers moving at different speeds to detect cycles, find midpoints, and solve linked list problems.
learningObjectives: Detect cycles in O(1) space using Floyd's algorithm.
Find the start of a cycle.
Find the middle of a linked list in one pass.
overview.interviewRelevance: Cycle detection and midpoint-finding are top-tier linked list interview problems.

paragraphs: Deadlock detection in operating systems uses cycle detection in resource allocation graphs.
Finding the duplicate number in an array (where values are in range 1..N) can be solved with Floyd's cycle detection.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: If a fast pointer (2× speed) ever meets a slow pointer, there MUST be a cycle — they can't meet on a straight path.

definition.technicalDefinition: Two pointers traverse a sequence at different speeds (typically 1 step vs 2 steps). If a cycle exists, the fast pointer will eventually catch up to the slow pointer.

vocabulary.definition: Advances one step at a time.
Advances two steps at a time.
Phase 1: detect cycle (meeting point). Phase 2: find cycle start.

keyProperties: Cycle detection: O(N) time, O(1) space.
Finding middle: when fast reaches end, slow is at the middle.
Finding cycle start: reset one pointer to head, advance both at speed 1.

operations.name: Detect Cycle
operations.description: Move slow by 1 and fast by 2. If they meet, there's a cycle. If fast reaches null, there's no cycle.
operations.visualNote: Two pointers advance at different speeds along the linked list, meeting inside the cycle.

operations.name: Find Middle
operations.description: When fast reaches the end, slow is at the middle of the list.
operations.visualNote: Slow pointer ends up in the middle when fast reaches the end.

practice.title: Linked List Cycle
practice.keyInsight: Fast and slow meet → cycle exists.

practice.title: Linked List Cycle II
practice.keyInsight: Phase 2: reset one to head, advance both by 1, they meet at cycle start.

practice.title: Middle of the Linked List
practice.keyInsight: Slow pointer is at middle when fast reaches end.

practice.title: Find the Duplicate Number
practice.keyInsight: Model the array as a linked list where val = next index, find cycle.

practice.title: Happy Number
practice.keyInsight: Digit-square sequence forms a linked list — detect if it cycles back to non-1.

conceptQuiz.question: Why does the fast-and-slow technique guarantee cycle detection?
options: The fast pointer closes the gap by 1 each step, so they must meet inside the cycle
It uses extra memory to track visited nodes
conceptQuiz.explanation: In each iteration, the distance between fast and slow decreases by 1, guaranteeing they meet within the cycle.

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Given a singly linked list, determine if it contains a cycle, using O(1) extra space.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: O(1) space cycle detection is the defining application of Floyd's Tortoise and Hare algorithm.
